segments.text: Then Job replied:
Indeed, I know that this is true. But how can mere mortals prove their innocence before God?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Though they wished to dispute with him, they could not answer him one time out of a thousand.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: His wisdom is profound, his power is vast. Who has resisted him and come out unscathed?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He moves mountains without their knowing it and overturns them in his anger.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He shakes the earth from its place and makes its pillars tremble.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He speaks to the sun and it does not shine; he seals off the light of the stars.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He alone stretches out the heavens and treads on the waves of the sea.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He is the Maker of the Bear and Orion, the Pleiades and the constellations of the south.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He performs wonders that cannot be fathomed, miracles that cannot be counted.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: When he passes me, I cannot see him; when he goes by, I cannot perceive him.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If he snatches away, who can stop him? Who can say to him, 'What are you doing?'
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: God does not restrain his anger; even the cohorts of Rahab cowered at his feet.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: How then can I dispute with him? How can I find words to argue with him?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Though I were innocent, I could not answer him; I could only plead with my Judge for mercy.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Even if I summoned him and he responded, I do not believe he would give me a hearing.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He would crush me with a storm and multiply my wounds for no reason.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He would not let me catch my breath but would overwhelm me with misery.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If it is a matter of strength, he is mighty! And if it is a matter of justice, who can challenge him?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Even if I were innocent, my mouth would condemn me; if I were blameless, it would pronounce me guilty.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Although I am blameless, I have no concern for myself; I despise my own life.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: It is all the same; that is why I say, 'He destroys both the blameless and the wicked.'
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: When a scourge brings sudden death, he mocks the despair of the innocent.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: When a land falls into the hands of the wicked, he blindfolds its judges. If it is not he, then who is it?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My days are swifter than a runner; they fly away without a glimpse of joy.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: They skim past like boats of papyrus, like eagles swooping down on their prey.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If I say, 'I will forget my complaint, I will change my expression, and smile,'
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I still dread all my sufferings, for I know you will not hold me innocent.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Since I am already found guilty, why should I struggle in vain?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Even if I washed myself with soap and my hands with cleansing powder,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: you would plunge me into a slime pit so that even my clothes would detest me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He is not a mere mortal like me that I might answer him, that we might confront each other in court.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If only there were someone to mediate between us, someone to bring us together,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: someone to remove God's rod from me, so that his terror would frighten me no more.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Then I would speak up without fear of him, but as it now stands with me, I cannot.
segments.speaker: Job
